author: John Chrysostom

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When children are just brought to their learning, their teachers do not give them many tasks in succession, nor do they set them once for all, but they often repeat to them the same short ones, so that what is said may be easily implanted in their minds, and they may not be vexed at the first onset with the quantity, and with finding it hard to remember, and become less active in picking up what is given them, a kind of sluggishness arising from the difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I, who wish to effect the same with you, and to render your labor easy, take by little and little the food which lies on this Divine table, and instill it into your souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account I shall handle again the same words, not so as to say again the same things, but to set before you only what yet remains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Come, then, let us again apply our discourse to the introduction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, when all the other Evangelists had begun with the Dispensation ; (for Matthew says, “The Book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the Son of David”; and Luke too relates to us in the beginning of his Gospel the events relating to Mary; and in like manner Mark dwells on the same narratives, from that point detailing to us the history of the Baptist;) why, when they began with these matters, did John briefly and in a later place hint at them, saying, “the Word was made flesh” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 .); and, passing by everything else, His conception, His birth, His bringing up, His growth, at once discourse to us concerning His Eternal Generation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I will now tell you what the reason of this is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the other Evangelists had dwelt most on the accounts of His coming in the flesh, there was fear lest some, being of grovelling minds, might for this reason rest in these doctrines alone, as indeed was the case with Paul of Samosata.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order, therefore, to lead away from this fondness for earth those who were like to fall into it, and to draw them up towards heaven, with good reason he commences his narrative from above, and from the eternal subsistence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For while Matthew enters upon his relation from Herod the king, Luke from Tiberius Cæsar, Mark from the Baptism of John, this Apostle, leaving alone all these things, ascends beyond all time or age.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thither darting forward the imagination of his hearers to the “was in the beginning,” not allowing it to stay at any point, nor setting any limit, as they did in Herod, and Tiberius, and John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what we may mention besides as especially deserving our admiration is, that John, though he gave himself up to the higher doctrine, yet did not neglect the Dispensation; nor were the others, though intent upon the relation of this, silent as to the subsistence before the ages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good cause; for One Spirit It was that moved the souls of all; and therefore they have shown great unanimity in their narrative.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But thou, beloved, when thou hast heard of “The Word,” do not endure those who say, that He is a work; nor those even who think, that He is simply a word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For many are the words of God which angels execute, but of those words none is God; they all are prophecies or commands, (for in Scripture it is usual to call the laws of God His commands, and prophecies, words; wherefore in speaking of the angels, he says, “Mighty in strength, fulfilling His word”) ( Ps. ciii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 20 ), but this Word is a Being with subsistence, proceeding without affection from the Father Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this, as I before said, he has shown by the term “Word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As therefore the expression, “In the beginning was the Word,” shows His Eternity, so “was in the beginning with God,” has declared to us His Co-eternity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that you may not, when you hear “In the beginning was the Word,” suppose Him to be Eternal, and yet imagine the life of the Father to differ from His by some interval and longer duration, and so assign a beginning to the Only-Begotten, he adds, “was in the beginning with God”; so eternally even as the Father Himself, for the Father was never without the Word, but He was always God with God, yet Each in His proper Person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then, one says, does John assert, that He was in the world, if He was with God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He was both with God and in the world also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither Father nor Son are limited in any way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since, if “there is no end of His greatness” ( Ps. cxlv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ), and if “of His wisdom there is no number” ( Ps. cxlvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), it is clear that there cannot be any beginning in time to His Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, that “In the beginning God made the heaven and the earth” ( Gen. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ); what dost thou understand from this “beginning”? clearly, that they were created before all visible things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So, respecting the Only-Begotten, when you hear that He was “in the beginning,” conceive of him as before all intelligible things, and before the ages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if any one say, “How can it be that He is a Son, and yet not younger than the Father? since that which proceeds from something else needs must be later than that from which it proceeds”; we will say that, properly speaking, these are human reasonings; that he who questions on this matter will question on others yet more improper; and that to such we ought not even to give ear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For our speech is now concerning God, not concerning the nature of men, which is subject to the sequence and necessary conclusions of these reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still, for the assurance of the weaker sort, we will speak even to these points.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, then, does the radiance of the sun proceed from the substance itself of the sun, or from some other source?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Any one not deprived of his very senses needs must confess, that it proceeds from the substance itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet, although the radiance proceeds from the sun itself, we cannot say that it is later in point of time than the substance of that body, since the sun has never appeared without its rays.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if in the case of these visible and sensible bodies there has been shown to be something which proceeds from something else, and yet is not after that from whence it proceeds; why are you incredulous in the case of the invisible and ineffable Nature?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This same thing there takes place, but in a manner suitable to That Substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is for this reason that Paul too calls Him “Brightness” ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); setting forth thereby His being from Him and His Co-eternity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, tell me, were not all the ages, and every interval created by Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Any man not deprived of his senses must necessarily confess this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is no interval therefore between the Son and the Father; and if there be none, then He is not after, but Co-eternal with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “before” and “after” are notions implying time, since, without age or time, no man could possibly imagine these words; but God is above times and ages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if in any case you say that you have found a beginning to the Son, see whether by the same reason and argument you are not compelled to reduce the Father also to a beginning, earlier indeed, but still a beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when you have assigned to the Son a limit and beginning of existence, do you not proceed upwards from that point, and say, that the Father was before it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Clearly you do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me then, what is the extent of the Father’s prior subsistence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For whether you say that the interval is little, or whether you say it is great, you equally have brought the Father to a beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is clear, that it is by measuring the space that you say whether it is little or great; yet it would not be possible to measure it, unless there were a beginning on either side; so that as far as you are concerned you have given the Father a beginning, and henceforth, according to your argument, not even the Father will be without beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See you that the word spoken by the Saviour is true, and the saying everywhere discovers its force?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is that word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is “He that honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father.” ( John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I know indeed that what now has been said cannot by many be comprehended, and therefore it is that in many places we avoid agitating questions of human reasonings, because the rest of the people cannot follow such arguments, and if they could, still they have nothing firm or sure in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For the thoughts of mortal men are miserable, and our devices are but uncertain.” ( Wisd. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still I should like to ask our objectors, what means that which is said by the Prophet, “Before Me there was no God formed, nor is there any after Me”? ( Is. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the Son is younger than the Father, how, says He, “Nor is there any after me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will you take away the being of the Only-Begotten Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: You either must dare this, or admit one Godhead with distinct Persons of the Father and Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Finally, how could the expression, “All things were made by Him,” be true?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if there is an age older than He, how can that which was before Him have been made by Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See ye to what daring the argument has carried them, when once the truth has been unsettled?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did not the Evangelist say, that He was made from things that were not, as Paul declares of all things, when he says, “Who calleth those things which be not as though they were”; but says, “Was in the beginning”? ( Rom. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is contrary to that; and with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God neither is made, nor has anything older; these are words of the Greeks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me this too: Would you not say, that the Creator beyond all comparison excels His works?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet since that which is from things that were not is similar to them, where is the superiority not admitting of comparison?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what mean the expressions, “I am the first and I am the last” ( Is. xliv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ); and, “before Me was no other God formed”? ( Is. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the Son be not of the same Essence, there is another God; and if He be not Co-eternal, He is after Him; and if He did not proceed from His Essence, clear it is that He was made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if they assert, that these things were said to distinguish Him from idols, why do they not allow that it is to distinguish Him from idols that he says, “the Only True God”? ( John xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, if this was said to distinguish Him from idols, how would you interpret the whole sentence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After Me,” He says, “is no other God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In saying this, He does not exclude the Son, but that “After Me there is no idol God,” not that “there is no Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Allowed, says he; what then? and the expression, “Before Me was no other God formed,” will you so understand, as that no idol God indeed was formed before Him, but yet a Son was formed before Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What evil spirit would assert this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I do not suppose that even Satan himself would do so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, if He be not Co-eternal with the Father, how can you say that His Life is infinite?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if it have a beginning from before, although it be endless, yet it is not infinite; for the infinite must be infinite in both directions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Paul also declared, when he said, “Having neither beginning of days, nor end of life” ( Heb. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); by this expression showing that He is both without beginning and without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the one has no limit, so neither has the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In one direction there is no end, in the other no beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how again, since He is “Life,” was there ever when He was not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all must allow, that Life both is always, and is without beginning and without end, if It be indeed Life, as indeed It is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if there be when It is not, how can It be the life of others, when It even Itself is not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” says one, “does John lay down a beginning by saying, ‘In the beginning was’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, have you attended to the “In the beginning,” and to the “was,” and do you not understand the expression, “the Word was”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What! when the Prophet says, “From everlasting and to everlasting Thou art” ( Ps. xc.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ), does he say this to assign Him limits?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, but to declare His Eternity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider now that the case is the same in this place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He did not use the expression as assigning limits, since he did not say, “had a beginning,” but “was in the beginning”; by the word “was” carrying thee forward to the idea that the Son is without beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet observe,” says he, “the Father is named with the addition of the article, but the Son without it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then, when the Apostle says, “The Great God, and our Saviour Jesus Christ” ( Tit. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ); and again, “Who is above all, God”? ( Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is true that here he has mentioned the Son, without the article; but he does the same with the Father also, at least in his Epistle to the Philippians ( c. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), he says, “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God”; and again to the Romans, “Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.” ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, it was superfluous for it to be attached in that place, when close above it was continually attached to “the Word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as in speaking concerning the Father, he says, “God is a Spirit” ( John iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ), and we do not, because the article is not joined to “Spirit,” yet deny the Spiritual Nature of God; so here, although the article is not annexed to the Son, the Son is not on that account a less God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because in saying “God,” and again “God,” he does not reveal to us any difference in this Godhead, but the contrary; for having before said, “and the Word was God”; that no one might suppose the Godhead of the Son to be inferior, he immediately adds the characteristics of genuine Godhead, including Eternity, (for “He was,” says he, “in the beginning with God,”) and attributing to Him the office of Creator.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “by Him were all things made, and without Him was not anything made that was made”; which His Father also everywhere by the Prophets declares to be especially characteristic of His own Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Prophets are continually busy on this kind of demonstration, not only of itself, but when they contend against the honor shown to idols; “Let the gods perish,” says one “who have not made heaven and earth” ( Jer. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ): and again, “I have stretched out the heaven with My hand” ( Is. xliv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ); and it is as declaring it to be indicative of Divinity, that He everywhere puts it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Evangelist himself was not satisfied with these words, but calls Him “Life” too and “Light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now He was ever with the Father, if He Himself created all things, if He brought all things into existence, and keeps together all things, (for, this he meant by “Life,”) if He enlightens all things, who so senseless as to say, that the Evangelist desired to teach an inferiority of Divinity by those very expressions, by which, rather than by any others, it is possible to express its equality and not differing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then confound the creation with the Creator, lest we too hear it said of us, that “they served the creature rather than the Creator” ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ); for although it be asserted that this is said of the heavens, still in speaking of the heavens he positively says, that we must not serve the creature, for it is a heathenish thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore not lay ourselves under this curse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this the Son of God came, that He might rid us from this service; for this He took the form of a slave, that He might free us from this slavery; for this He was spit upon, for this He was buffeted, for this He endured the shameful death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not, I entreat you, make all these things of none effect, let us not go back to our former unrighteousness, or rather to unrighteousness much more grievous; for to serve the creature is not the same thing as to bring down the Creator, as far at least as in us lies, to the meanness of the creature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He continues being such as He is; as says the Psalmist, “Thou art the same, and Thy years shall not fail.” ( Ps. cii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then glorify Him as we have received from our fathers, let us glorify Him both by our faith and by our works; for sound doctrines avail us nothing to salvation, if our life is corrupt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then order it according to what is well-pleasing to God, setting ourselves far from all filthiness, unrighteousness, and covetousness, as strangers and foreigners and aliens to the things here on earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any have much wealth and possessions, let him so use them as one who is a sojourner, and who, whether he will or not, shall shortly pass from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If one be injured by another, let him not be angry forever, nay rather not even for a time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Apostle has not allowed us more than a single day for the venting of anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let not,” says he, “the sun go down upon your wrath” ( Eph. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ); and with reason; for it is matter for contentment that even in so short a time nothing unpleasant take place; but if night also overtake us, what has happened becomes more grievous, because the fire of our wrath is increased ten thousand times by memory, and we at our leisure enquire into it more bitterly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before therefore we obtain this pernicious leisure and kindle a hotter fire, he bids us arrest beforehand and quench the mischief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the passion of wrath is fierce, fiercer than any flame; and so we need much haste to prevent the flame, and not allow it to blaze up high, for so this disease becomes a cause of many evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It has overturned whole houses, it has dissolved old companionships, and has worked tragedies not to be remedied in a short moment of time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For,” saith one, “the sway of his fury shall be his destruction.” ( Ecclus. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then leave such a wild beast unbridled, but put upon him a muzzle in all ways strong, the fear of the judgment to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whenever a friend grieves thee, or one of thine own family exasperates thee, think of the sins thou hast committed against God, and that by kindness towards him thou makest that judgment more lenient to thyself, (“Forgive,” saith He, “and ye shall be forgiven”) ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 37 ), and thy passion shall quickly skulk away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And besides, consider this, whether there has been a time when thou wert being carried away into ferocity, and didst control thyself, and another time when thou hast been dragged along by the passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Compare the two seasons, and thou shalt gain thence great improvement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, when didst thou praise thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was it when thou wast worsted, or when thou hadst the mastery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do we not in the first case vehemently blame ourselves, and feel ashamed. even when none reproves us, and do not many feelings of repentance come over us, both for what we have said and done; but when we gain the mastery, then are we not proud, and exult as conquerors?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For victory in the case of anger is, not the requiting evil with the like, (that is utter defeat,) but the bearing meekly to be ill treated and ill spoken of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To get the better is not to inflict but to suffer evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when angry do not say, “certainly I will retaliate,” “certainly I will be revenged”; do not persist in saying to those who exhort you to gain a victory, “I will not endure that the man mock me, and escape clear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He will never mock thee, except when thou avengest thyself; or if he even should mock thee he will do so as a fool.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seek not when thou conquerest honor from fools, but consider that sufficient which comes from men of understanding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, why do I set before thee a small and mean body of spectators, when I make it up of men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Look up straight to God: He will praise thee, and the man who is approved by Him must not seek honor from mortals.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Mortal honor often arises from flattery or hatred of others, and brings no profit; but the decision of God is free from this inequality, and brings great advantage to the man whom He approves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This praise then let us follow after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will you learn what an evil is anger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Stand by while others are quarreling in the forum.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In yourself you cannot easily see the disgrace of the thing, because your reason is darkened and drunken; but when you are clear from the passion, and while your judgment is sound, view your own case in others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, I pray you, the crowds collecting round, and the angry men like maniacs acting shamefully in the midst.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when the passion boils up within the breast, and becomes excited and savage, the mouth breathes fire, the eyes emit fire, all the face becomes swollen, the hands are extended disorderly, the feet dance ridiculously, and they spring at those who restrain them, and differ nothing from madmen in their insensibility to all these things; nay, differ not from wild asses, kicking and biting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Truly a passionate man is not a graceful one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And then, when after this exceedingly ridiculous conduct, they return home and come to themselves, they have the greater pain, and much fear, thinking who were present when they were angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For like raving men, they did not then know the standers by, but when they have returned to their right mind, then they consider, were they friends? were they foes and enemies that looked on?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they fear alike about both; the first because they will condemn them and give them more shame; the others because they will rejoice at it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if they have even exchanged blows, then their fear is the more pressing; for instance, lest anything very grievous happen to the sufferer; a fever follow and bring on death, or a troublesome swelling rise and place him in danger of the worst.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, “what need” (say they) “had I of fighting, and violence, and quarreling?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perish such things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And then they curse the ill-fated business which caused them to begin, and the more foolish lay on “wicked spirits,” and “an evil hour,” the blame of what has been done; but these things are not from an evil hour, (for there is no such thing as an evil hour,) nor from a wicked spirit, but from the wickedness of those captured by the passion; they draw the spirits to them, and bring upon themselves all things terrible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the heart swells,” says one, “and is stung by insults.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know it; and that is the reason why I admire those who master this dreadful wild beast; yet it is possible if we will, to beat off the passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For why when our rulers insult us do we not feel it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is because fear counterbalances the passion, and frightens us from it, and does not allow it to spring up at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why too do our servants, though insulted by us in ten thousand ways, bear all in silence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they too have the same restraint laid upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And think thou not merely of the fear of God, but that it is even God Himself who then insults thee, who bids thee be silent, and then thou wilt bear all things meekly, and say to the aggressor, How can I be angry with thee? there is another that restrains both my hand and my tongue; and the saying will be a suggestion of sound wisdom, both to thyself and to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even now we bear unbearable things on account of men, and often say to those who have insulted us, “Such an one insulted me, not you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall we not use the same caution in the case of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How else can we hope for pardon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us say to our soul, “It is God who holds our hands, who now insults us; let us not be restive, let not God be less honored by us than men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did ye shudder at the word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I wish you would shudder not at the word only, but at the deed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God hath commanded us when buffeted not only to endure it, but even to offer ourselves to suffer something worse; and we withstand Him with such vehemence, that we not only refuse to offer ourselves to suffer evil, but even avenge ourselves, nay often are the first to act on the offensive, and think we are disgraced if we do not the same in return.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yes, and the mischief is, that when utterly worsted we think ourselves conquerors, and when lying undermost and receiving ten thousand blows from the devil, then we imagine that we are mastering him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, I exhort you, understand what is the nature of this victory, and this kind of nature let us follow after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To suffer evil is to get the crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then we wish to be proclaimed victors by God, let us not in these contests observe the laws of heathen games, but those of God, and learn to bear all things with longsuffering; for so we may get the better of our antagonists, and obtain both present and promised goods, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom and with whom to the Father and the Holy Spirit be glory, power, and honor, now and ever, and world without end.
